clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) and evidence of acute kidney injury (AKI Stage 1) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. Thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) suggests coagulopathy, while bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) indicates hepatic stress. Hemoglobin of 9.8 g/dL raises concern for anemia. Rapid creatinine rise to 8.9 mg/dL was excluded as outlier but AKI Stage 1 (2.1 mg/dL) remains.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and AKI meet Sepsis-3 criteria (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
Creatinine elevation to 2.1 mg/dL (≥0.3 mg/dL increase) meets KDIGO Stage 1 criteria
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or urine output <0.5 mL/kg/hr for ≥6h

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets ≤95 K/uL meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring threshold

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis bundle: obtain cultures, administer broad-spectrum antibiotics
Assess for source of infection (imaging, clinical exam)
Monitor AKI progression with hourly urine output and creatinine trends
Consider platelet transfusion if thrombocytopenia <50 K/uL or active bleeding
Reassess fluid status and vasopressor requirements for hypotension
doctor_handoff: Review for new infection sources, confirm sepsis bundle completion, and evaluate need for renal replacement therapy given AKI Stage 1 progression.

disease_progression.observation: Baseline abnormalities: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
